query: How much does hotel construction cost in Texas?
base_value:
From projects we delivered

Home2Suites by Hilton, Comfort Suites & Holiday Inn Express

Richmond, Pasadena & Pflugerville, TX · 90,500 / new construction / 114,700 sq ft · Hospitality · Construction management

Three Texas hotel deliveries: a 90,500 sq ft Home2Suites by Hilton in Richmond, a Comfort Suites in Pasadena, and a 114,700 sq ft Holiday Inn Express in Pflugerville. The cost-per-key economics and brand-prototype requirements covered in this guide reflect what these projects actually required to deliver on time and on budget.

Introduction
answer_block:
In Texas in 2026, hotel construction costs range from approximately $184,000 to $305,000 per key depending on hotel type, with limited-service hotels running $220,000–$246,000 per key and extended-stay upscale properties reaching $279,000–$305,000 per key. On a per-square-foot basis, budgets typically fall between $175 and $275 per SF for hard costs alone. These Texas figures run 12–15% above national averages due to MEP labor scarcity, coastal wind-load requirements, and elevated FF&E costs.

  • Limited-Service (Texas 2026): $220,193–$245,562 per key
  • Extended Stay – Midscale (Texas 2026): $184,480–$215,451 per key
  • Extended Stay – Upscale (Texas 2026): $278,787–$304,700 per key
